5	## Priority Rule

10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.

17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.

21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.

The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)

105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. 23	    directive that asks the writer to add ``\leanok`` puts it in a rule
24	    conflict; the writer must obey its descriptor, so the "verification"
25	    instruction will appear to fail until sync_leanok runs. Leave
26	    ``\leanok`` out of the writer's directive entirely.
27	  - **``\mathlibok`` is the exception — you MAY direct it.** When a
28	    declaration the chapter depends on is provided by Mathlib as-is, it
29	    is good practice to have the writer author an explicit *Mathlib
30	    dependency anchor*: a block that states the Mathlib result (in the
31	    project's notation), carries ``\lean{}`` naming the real Mathlib
32	    declaration, and is marked ``\mathlibok`` so the DAG treats it as
33	    done and the route's reliance on Mathlib stays visible. Name such
34	    anchors in the directive (statement + the Mathlib ``\lean{}``
35	    target). The writer marks ``\mathlibok`` ONLY on these anchors —
36	    never on the project's own to-be-proved declarations.
37	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
38	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
39	    writer with TWO globs:
40	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
41	      --write-domain 'references/**'
42	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
43	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
44	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
45	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
46	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
47	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
48	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
49	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
50	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
51	  - After a significant writer round, re-run the blueprint review
52	    in the same iteration (the relevant entry in your catalog) to
53	    confirm the updated blueprint is now sufficient — do not assume
54	    the writer fixed everything.
55	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
56	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
57	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
58	    strategy-level issue.
59	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
60	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
61	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.

63	
64	# Blueprint Writer
65	
66	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
67	
68	## Your Job
69	
70	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
71	
72	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Blueprint Writer Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Target chapter
83	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
84	
85	## Strategy context
86	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
87	
88	## Required content
89	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
90	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
91	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
92	- ...
93	
94	## Out of scope
95	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
96	
97	## References
98	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
99	- ...
100	
101	## Expected outcome
102	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
103	```
104	
105	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
106	
107	## Chapter format
108	
109	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
110	
111	```latex
112	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
113	  \label{thm:some_label}
114	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
115	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
116	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174  (read from references/hartshorne-III-5.md)
117	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A morphism $f: X \to Y$ of schemes locally of finite
118	  % type is said to be smooth at $x \in X$ if there exist an open affine
119	  % neighborhood $V = \Spec B$ of $f(x)$ and an open affine neighborhood
120	  % $U = \Spec A$ of $x$ with $f(U) \subset V$ such that ..."
121	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, III.5.1.}
122	  Informal statement of the theorem, in the project's notation.
123	\end{theorem}
124	
125	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Proof. We may assume $Y = \Spec B$ and
126	% $X = \Spec A$ are affine. Then $f$ corresponds to a ring homomorphism
127	% $\varphi: B \to A$, and $f$ is smooth at $x$ if and only if ..."
128	\begin{proof}
129	  \uses{thm:another_result}
130	  Step-by-step informal proof, in the project's notation. Reference blueprint labels
131	  with \uses{...} so the dependency graph stays accurate.
132	\end{proof}
133	```
134	
135	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
136	
137	### Citation discipline (the hard rule)
138	
139	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**
140	
141	1. **`% SOURCE:` LaTeX comment** — pointer + local file. Format: `% SOURCE: <citation pointer> (read from references/<file>.md)`. The pointer is the source identifier + section/theorem/definition number + page when available (e.g. `[Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174` or `[Stacks Project], Tag 01V4`). The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical names the local file you opened to produce the verbatim quote.
142	2. **`% SOURCE QUOTE:` LaTeX comment** — the **verbatim text** of the cited statement, copied character-by-character from the local reference file you just named in the `% SOURCE:` line. Verbatim means:
143	   - **Original language.** French for Bourbaki / EGA, German for Grothendieck's early notes, English for Hartshorne / Vakil / Stacks, etc. Do NOT translate.
144	   - **Original notation preserved.** If the source writes $\mathcal{O}_X^*$ and the project uses $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$, the verbatim quote keeps $\mathcal{O}_X^*$. The project-notation restatement happens in the prose body that follows, NOT in the quote.
145	   - **Every word, every symbol.** No paraphrase, no "for brevity" abbreviation. The quote is the anti-hallucination signal — a writer who actually opened the source can paste; a writer reconstructing from memory cannot reproduce the source's exact words.
146	3. **Visible `\textit{Source: <pointer>.}` line** — first line of the prose body. Renders in the PDF so a mathematician reading the typeset blueprint sees the citation at a glance.
147	
148	**For proof blocks**: include a `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` LaTeX comment **immediately before** the `\begin{proof}` environment (NOT inside it). Same verbatim rules — original language, original notation, every word. The informal proof body inside `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}` is the project's restated version in project notation, what the prover formalizes.
149	
150	When a source proof is too long for one verbatim block (multi-page construction): split the theorem into sub-lemmas in the directive's logical structure, and give each sub-statement its own `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`. One opaque mega-quote defeats verifiability. If even sub-splitting is impractical, report it in "Notes for Plan Agent" — do not silently drop the verbatim quote.
151	
152	**For Archon-original / project-bespoke results** (the directive does not name an external source for this block): the source lines are omitted — the block stands on the proof sketch alone.
153	
154	**The hard rule, explicit:**
155	
156	> **You may NEVER write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from training memory.** Every such line must be backed by a local file under `references/` that you have **opened and read in this session**, and the verbatim quote must be a character-by-character copy of text from that file.
157	
158	The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical in `% SOURCE:` is your discipline check. If, when reviewing your own draft, you find a block where you cannot point to the specific local file you read to produce the quote — you fabricated it. Delete the quote. Either dispatch a `reference-retriever` (see below) to obtain the missing source, or report the block as INCOMPLETE under "Notes for Plan Agent".
159	
160	If the directive named a source but the local reference file is missing or doesn't contain the specific statement you need:
161	
162	- **Dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** (see "Dispatching a reference-retriever" below) to fetch the missing material into `references/<slug>.md`.
163	- **Wait for the retriever to return**, then **open and read the new file**.
164	- THEN write the citation block, citing the new local file in the `% SOURCE:` parenthetical.
165	
166	If retrieval fails (paywall, broken link, no API key, not available online): mark the block `% SOURCE: <pointer> (verbatim text not yet retrieved)` and skip it. Report it as INCOMPLETE. Do NOT substitute a paraphrase, a recollection, or a translation as the verbatim quote — that's the iter-149 failure mode and the entire rule exists to prevent it.
167	
168	## Rules
169	
170	### What you CAN do
171	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
172	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
173	- **Add, correct, or complete `\uses{...}` cross-references** so the dependency edges match the real mathematics. If your chapter's proof of `T` invokes lemma `L`, `T` must declare `\uses{lem:L}`; if a `\uses{}` points at a label that no longer exists, fix it. Verify with `leandag` (see "Verifying dependencies with leandag" below) — do not eyeball it.
174	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
175	- **Write entries for Lean helper declarations.** The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: when the directive lists a helper that exists only in Lean (a prover-created lemma, an instance, a decomposition step), give it a real entry — concise informal statement, `\label{}`, `\lean{}` with the exact Lean name, accurate `\uses{}`, and at least a one-line informal proof. "It's trivial" is not a reason to skip it: the entry is what carries the dependency edges and what helps a later prover fill the sorry. When the helper's Lean proof relies on a fact with no blueprint entry, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" (or create it too, if it belongs in your chapter).
176	- **Author a *Mathlib dependency anchor* and mark it `\mathlibok`** when your chapter relies on a result Mathlib already provides — write out the statement and point `\lean{}` at the real Mathlib declaration (see "Mathlib dependency anchors" below). This is the only marker you may add.
177	- **Remove a declaration block ONLY when the directive explicitly lists it for removal** (typically an isolated/orphaned statement the reviewer flagged `remove`). Default to wiring an isolated node into the graph (add the missing `\uses{}`), not deleting it — an isolated node is usually a missing edge, not dead weight. Never remove the project goal, and never remove a block whose `\lean{}` still names a live Lean declaration.
178	- Read `references/summary.md` and any reference that is in `references/` to ground your writing in the project's sources.
179	
180	### What you MUST do
181	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
182	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
183	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
184	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
185	- **Follow citation discipline** for every block derived from external reference material (see "Citation discipline (the hard rule)" above). Each such block needs `% SOURCE:` with a local-file parenthetical, `% SOURCE QUOTE:` with a verbatim original-language quote, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` before the proof env when applicable, and a visible `\textit{Source: ...}` prefix. Never cite from memory — only from a local `references/<file>.md` you opened and read in this session.
186	- **Document every change** in your report, including which `references/<file>.md` files you opened (under "References consulted").
187	
188	### What you MUST NOT do
189	- **Do NOT reference project history.** No "since iteration N," "after our failed attempt," or session narrative. The blueprint must read as a standalone mathematical document.
190	- **Do NOT include Lean implementation details.** No tactic suggestions, typeclass wiring notes, or Lean code structure hints in the prose. If a Lean note is needed, it belongs in `.lean` files (the plan agent will use `lean-scaffolder` for that).
191	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` markers.** `\leanok` is earned by a sorry-free Lean proof and set by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase — never by you. (`\mathlibok` is different: you MAY add it, but ONLY on a Mathlib dependency anchor — see "Mathlib dependency anchors" below — never on the project's own to-be-proved declarations.)
192	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
193	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
194	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
195	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
196	- **Do NOT write literal `REF` placeholders.** Never produce prose like "Definition~REF" or "Sections REF–REF". Cross-reference declarations with `\cref{<label>}` using the real label (look it up in the chapter or via `leandag`); when the target genuinely has no label yet, describe it by name in prose. The blueprint-doctor flags every literal `REF` as a malformed reference.
197	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
198	- **Do NOT fabricate citations.** Never write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from memory. If you don't have a local `references/<file>.md` containing the source text, dispatch a retriever and wait. The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical is the discipline check — if you cannot truthfully point to the file you read, the citation block is not allowed in the chapter.
199	- **Do NOT translate or restate the verbatim quote.** `% SOURCE QUOTE:` and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` contain the source's original language, original notation, every word as-is. Project-notation rewrites belong in the rendered prose body, not in the verbatim comment.
200	
201	## Mathlib dependency anchors (`\mathlibok`)
202	
203	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:
204	
205	- makes the reliance explicit and readable (a mathematician sees exactly which Mathlib result the route stands on);
206	- gives `leandag` a real node, so the `\uses{}` resolves (no broken/unknown ref) and the node's effort is **done** rather than ∞;
207	- keeps it clear how much the route leans on Mathlib vs. on original work.
208	
209	Write the anchor like any statement block, with three differences:
210	
211	```latex
212	\begin{lemma}[Hensel's lemma]
213	  \label{lem:hensel_mathlib}
214	  \lean{Mathlib.RingTheory.Henselian.foo}   % the REAL Mathlib declaration
215	  \mathlibok
216	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
217	  Statement of the result in the project's notation.
218	\end{lemma}
219	```
220	
221	- **`\lean{}` names the actual Mathlib declaration** (with its real namespace), so `leandag` matches the node to Mathlib.
222	- **`\mathlibok`** marks it as supplied by Mathlib — the DAG treats it as done; no `\begin{proof}` is needed.
223	- No `% SOURCE:`/`% SOURCE QUOTE:` block — Mathlib is the source; the `\lean{}` target *is* the citation.
224	
225	**The anti-hallucination rule (mirrors citation discipline):** mark `\mathlibok` ONLY when the statement genuinely exists in Mathlib and your stated form is faithful to it. A wrong or invented `\mathlibok` is worse than an ∞ hole — the loop will treat a non-existent result as available and skip proving a real gap. So:
226	
227	- Prefer anchors the **directive names** (the plan agent/reviewer identified them as Mathlib results).
228	- If you are not confident the exact statement is in Mathlib, do **not** mark `\mathlibok` — write the block without it (or leave the `\uses{}` for the reviewer to resolve) and flag it under "Notes for Plan Agent" so the reviewer can verify against Lean.
229	- Never put `\mathlibok` on one of the project's **own** to-be-proved declarations. That marker is for Mathlib-supplied results only; `\leanok` (project proofs) is never yours to add.
230	
231	## Reading references
232	
233	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
234	
235	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
236	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
237	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
238	
239	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
240	
241	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
242	
243	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
244	
245	Conditions for dispatch:
246	
247	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
248	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
249	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
250	
251	Dispatch (Bash; treat as blocking and await the report; in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
252	
253	```
254	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
255	  --name reference-retriever \
256	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
257	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
258	  --write-domain 'references/**'
259	```
260	
261	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
262	
263	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
264	
265	## Workflow
266	
267	1. Read your directive completely.
268	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references. Track which local files you actually opened — you will need their paths for the `% SOURCE:` parenthetical.
269	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
270	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow. For each block that derives from an external source, identify the specific local `references/<file>.md` containing the verbatim statement (and proof, if applicable). If no local file covers the statement, list it as "retrieval needed" for the next step.
271	5. **For every "retrieval needed" item, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and **wait** for it to return. THEN open and read the newly-written `references/<slug>.md` before drafting the citing block. Do not draft citation blocks against pending or imagined sources.
272	6. Make the edits. For each citation block: copy `% SOURCE QUOTE:` and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` content character-by-character from the local reference file — original language, original notation, every word. Do not transcribe from a window of the file you "remember reading"; have the file open and copy.
273	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`). Spot-check that every `% SOURCE:` line has a non-empty `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical and that the named file exists.
274	8. **Verify dependencies with `leandag`** (see the section below): confirm your chapter introduced no broken `\uses{}` and left none of your blocks unintentionally isolated. Fix the edges and re-check.
275	9. Write your report. List under "References consulted" every local file you opened in step 2 and step 5.
276	
277	## Verifying dependencies with leandag
278	
279	`leandag` parses the real Lean ↔ blueprint dependency DAG, so use it to check your `\uses{}` instead of trusting your memory of the graph. It is read-only on the blueprint (it never edits your chapter); run it after you make edits:
280	
281	```
282	leandag build --json                       # report: unknown_uses, unmatched_lean, isolated count
283	leandag query --isolated --chapter <c>     # isolated nodes in your chapter (no edges in or out)
284	leandag show isolated                       # all isolated nodes, project-wide
285	```
286	
287	`leandag` is on the same PATH as `archon`; it is the one tool you use to query the DAG.
288	
289	What to do with the output, scoped to **your chapter**:
290	
291	- **`unknown_uses` naming a label in your chapter** → you wrote a `\uses{}` pointing at a label that doesn't exist. Fix the label, or — if the target genuinely belongs in your chapter and the directive calls for it — add the missing declaration. If the missing label lives in another chapter, leave a correct `\uses{}` to it and note it under "Notes for Plan Agent" (you don't edit that chapter).
292	- **A block you wrote shows up as isolated** → it has no `\uses{}` out and nothing uses it. This is almost always a *missing edge*: add the `\uses{}` that ties it to the declarations its statement/proof actually depends on (and check whether a sibling result should `\uses{}` it). Only treat an isolated block as removable when your directive explicitly authorized removing it.
293	- **Removal** (directive-authorized only): delete the orphaned block cleanly and record it in your report. Never remove the goal or a block whose `\lean{}` still names a live Lean declaration.
294	
295	Keep `leandag` edits inside your assigned chapter — fixing a dependency that lives in another chapter is a "Notes for Plan Agent" item, not a cross-chapter edit.

19	The genus-\(0\) arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
20	(\(J = \Spec k\)); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
21	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\)
22	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
23	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field \(k\), so the
24	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
25	O(-2)) = 0\(, hence \)df = 0\(, hence \)f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
26	characteristic-\(p\) Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant \(f\) can have \(df = 0\). The
27	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
28	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
29	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
30	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
31	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
32	derives the genus-\(0\) base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
33	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
35	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that \(\mathbb P^1\) is
36	\emph{already complete} and \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
37	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
38	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{\(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut} (the project's
39	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
40	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
41	\mathbb P^1\(, \)(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\( (the M\"obius scaling fixing both \)0$ and
42	\(\infty\)), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
43	G_m\( (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising \)f(0) = \eta_A\( and composing \)h := \sigma_\times
44	\fatsemi f\(, one applies additivity of \)\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
45	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
46	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) proper, \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and
47	base points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\). Because \(0\) is a \emph{fixed point} of
48	scaling, the \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)\) is
49	\emph{constant} at \(\eta_A\), so the decomposition collapses to \(h = p \fatsemi f\), i.e.\
50	\(f(\lambda x) = f(x)\). Specialising \(x = 1\) gives that \(f|_{\mathbb G_m}\) is constant, and since
51	\(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in \(\mathbb P^1\) with \(A\) separated, \(f\) is constant
52	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
53	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
54	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
55	\(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\), and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
56	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive
57	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility
58	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
59	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-\(0\) critical path. The corollary that a
60	pointed regular map of abelian
61	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
62	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-\(0\) path; Milne's
63	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
64	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-\(0\) critical path
65	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
66	gives that every morphism \(\mathbb P^1 \to A\) is constant
67	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-\(0\)
68	curves (\cref{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}, \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) over \(\bar k\)) yields the
69	headline \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
70	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
71	neither Serre duality nor the FGA \(\Pic\) representability engine, so the genus-\(0\) arm is
72	fully decoupled from Route~A.
73	\medskip
74	Throughout, \(\bar k\) is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
75	\emph{abelian variety} over \(\bar k\) is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
76	project's Lean encoding \(A\) is an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) carrying
77	\(\mathtt{GrpObj}\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\), \(\mathtt{Smooth}\), and \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\).

88	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
89	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a

92	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
93	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety, and let \(Y\), \(Z\) be any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
94	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism such that for some \(y_0 \in Y\) the image
95	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0 \in Z\). Then there is a morphism
96	  \(g \colon Y \to Z\) with \(f = g \circ p_2\), where \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
97	  projection; that is, \(f\) depends only on the \(Y\)-coordinate, and \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) for any
98	  fixed \(x_0 \in X\).
99	\end{lemma}

139	  composite of \(y \mapsto (x_0, y)\) with \(f\)). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety (in

144	  and set \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection
145	  \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a \emph{closed} map (this is the defining property of
146	  completeness/properness); hence \(G\), the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\), is closed in

148	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), so \(y_0 \notin G\). Therefore
149	  \(V := Y - G\) is a non-empty open subset of \(Y\).

151	  \(f(X \times \{y\}) \subseteq U\). Thus the complete (proper) variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is
152	  mapped by \(f\) into the \emph{affine} variety \(U\). A proper connected variety mapping to an
153	  affine variety has image a single point (a global regular function on a complete connected
154	  variety is constant). Hence \(f(X \times \{y\})\) is one point, necessarily
155	  \(f(x_0, y) = g(y)\). So for all \(x \in X\) and \(y \in V\),
156	  \[ f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g(y) = (g \circ p_2)(x, y). \]
157	  The two morphisms \(f\) and \(g \circ p_2\) thus agree on the non-empty open
158	  \(X \times V\), which is dense in the irreducible \(X \times Y\); as \(Z\) is separated they
159	  agree everywhere. \qedhere

230	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety and \(Y\), \(Z\) any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
231	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a point \(x_0 \in X\), and suppose given
232	  \(y_0 \in Y\), \(z_0 \in Z\) with the \emph{collapse hypothesis}

234	     \text{(in Lean: } \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathbf 1_X)\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi y_0) \fatsemi f
235	     = \mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi z_0\text{).} \]
236	  Then there is a non-empty open subset \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) on which \(f\) agrees, as a
237	  scheme morphism, with the collapsed map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\), where
238	  $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,
239	  (p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y)\( is the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$'' endomorphism. Concretely
240	  \(U = X \times V\) with \(V := Y - G\), \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(Z - U_0))\) for an affine open
241	  neighbourhood \(U_0\) of \(z_0\), and on \(U\) one has \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y)\).
242	  \medskip
243	  \noindent\textbf{The collapse hypothesis is load-bearing.} The non-emptiness of \(V\) is exactly
244	  the assertion \(y_0 \notin G\), and this holds \emph{because} $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}
245	  \subseteq U_0\( keeps the slice over \)y_0\( inside the affine \)U_0\(, away from \)F = Z - U_0$. A
246	  version of this lemma \emph{without} the collapse hypothesis is false: for \(X = Y = Z\) and
247	  \(f = p_1\) the first projection, \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f \colon (x, y) \mapsto x_0\)
248	  agree on no non-empty open (the agreement locus is contained in \(\{x = x_0\}\), which has empty
249	  interior in the irreducible \(X \times Y\)). Therefore the formal target \emph{must} carry the
250	  collapse hypothesis as an explicit antecedent; dropping it (as an earlier decomposition
251	  silently did) makes the deferred goal unsatisfiable.

253	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization note: algebraically closed base, and locally-of-finite-type
254	  source.} The prose already takes \(\bar k\) algebraically closed, but the formal statement must
255	  \emph{encode} two properties. First, the cohomology-free proof of the slice-constancy bridge
256	  (below) needs each per-closed-point residue field \(\kappa(y)\) to be \(\bar k\) itself, so that a
257	  proper integral slice has global sections \(\Gamma = \bar k\) and maps to a single \(\bar k\)-point of
258	  the affine \(U_0\); this requires \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\). Second, the globalisation of the

181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val
266	    rw [Over.id_left]
267	    simp
268	  map_comp := by
269	    intros T T' T'' φ ψ
270	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
271	    apply MonoidHom.ext
272	    intro u
273	    apply Units.ext
274	    change ((φ ≫ ψ).unop.left.appTop).hom u.val
275	      = ((ψ.unop.left.appTop).hom (((φ.unop.left.appTop).hom) u.val))
276	    rw [show (φ ≫ ψ).unop = ψ.unop ≫ φ.unop from rfl, Over.comp_left]
277	    simp
278	
279	/-- **Forward** of the per-`T` bijection: a morphism `f : T ⟶ Gm kbar` in `Over (Spec k̄)`
280	gives the unit in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` corresponding to the image of the standard generator
281	`t ∈ k̄[t, t⁻¹]` under the induced ring map.
282	
283	Concretely, `f.left.appTop : Γ(Spec(GmRing), ⊤) ⟶ Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; composing with
284	`ΓSpecIso.inv` gives a ring map `GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. The image of the algebra-map
285	generator `t ∈ GmRing` (a unit via `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`) is a unit
286	in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. -/
287	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_toFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
288	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (f : T ⟶ Gm kbar) : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ :=
289	  letI ringMap : (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)) ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
290	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
291	  Units.map ringMap.hom.toMonoidHom
292	    (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
293	      (S := GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)).unit
294	
295	/-- Helper for `gmHomEquiv_invFun`: the underlying scheme morphism factors over `Spec k̄`.
296	Substantive content: reduces to `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp` + `MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq`
297	+ `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` (the Γ⊣Spec unit triangle). -/
298	private lemma gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
299	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) :
300	    letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
301	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
302	    letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
303	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
304	    haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
305	      change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
306	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
307	      exact u.isUnit
308	    letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
309	      IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
310	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
311	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap)) ≫ (Gm kbar).hom = T.hom := by
312	  -- Set up local abbreviations matching the letI's of the goal.
313	  set structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
314	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
315	  set polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
316	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val) with polyMap_def
317	  have hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
318	    rw [polyMap_def, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
319	    exact u.isUnit
320	  set liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
321	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
322	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg with liftedMap_def
323	  -- Unfold `(Gm kbar).hom`.
324	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap) ≫
325	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
326	  -- Combine the Spec.maps.
327	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
328	  -- `liftedMap ∘ algebraMap kbar GmRing = structureRingMap.hom`.
329	  have halg : (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) =
330	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
331	        (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
332	    ext r
333	    exact (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) r).symm
334	  have hcomp : liftedMap.comp (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) = structureRingMap.hom := by
335	    change (IsLocalization.Away.lift _ hg).comp _ = _
336	    rw [halg, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
337	    ext r
338	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) r) = _
339	    rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
340	  rw [hcomp]
341	  -- Now: T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map structureRingMap.hom = T.hom
342	  -- structureRingMap = ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ T.hom.appTop  (as CommRingCat morphism)
343	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫
344	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
345	          T.hom.appTop).hom)) = T.hom
346	  -- CommRingCat.ofHom (...).hom = the original morphism (since ofHom and .hom are inverses)
347	  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
348	        T.hom.appTop).hom) = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop from
349	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom _]
350	  rw [Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality]
351	  -- Now: (T.hom ≫ (Spec _).toSpecΓ) ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso.inv) = T.hom
352	  rw [Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
353	
354	/-- **Backward** of the per-`T` bijection: a unit `u : Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` gives the morphism
355	`T ⟶ Gm kbar` defined by the ring map `k̄[t, t⁻¹] → Γ(T.left, ⊤)` sending `t ↦ u.val`.
356	
357	Concretely: build the polynomial-ring map `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)` via
358	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` (sending `X() ↦ u.val`); lift through the localization via
359	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` (using that `u.val` is a unit); convert to a scheme morphism
360	via `Spec.map`-then-`toSpecΓ`; bundle with the `Over`-commutativity proof. -/
361	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_invFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
362	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) : T ⟶ Gm kbar :=
363	  -- structure ring map kbar → Γ(T.left, ⊤), via T.hom.appTop and ΓSpecIso
364	  letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
365	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
366	  letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
367	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
368	  haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
369	    change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
370	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
371	    exact u.isUnit
372	  letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
373	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
374	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
375	  Over.homMk
376	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap))
377	    (gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver kbar T u)
378	
379	/-- Round-trip identity 1: `invFun ∘ toFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
380	the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
381	applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
382	private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
383	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
384	    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
385	  intro f
386	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
387	  -- Goal: (invFun (toFun f)).left = f.left as T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing).
388	  -- (a) `Over.w f` transferred through `Spec.appTop` + `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`:
389	  --     `structureRingMap = algebraMap kbar GmRing ≫ g_f` (as CommRingCat morphisms),
390	  --     where `g_f := (ΓSpecIso (GmRing)).inv ≫ f.left.appTop`.
391	  have hw : (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop =
392	      CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) ≫
393	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop) := by
394	    have hOver := Over.w f
395	    -- `(Gm kbar).hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))` by defeq.
396	    change f.left ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
397	      at hOver
398	    rw [← hOver, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
399	    set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
400	      rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]
401	  -- (b) Compare the two morphisms `T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing)` via `ext_to_Spec`.
402	  apply AlgebraicGeometry.ext_to_Spec
403	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
404	      (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T f)).left.appTop
405	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
406	  -- Reduce LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f}` (same chain as in `right_inv`).
407	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
408	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
409	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.lift
410	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop
411	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
412	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
413	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
414	  -- Goal: `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f} = g_f` as CommRingCat morphisms.
415	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
416	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, IsLocalization.Away.lift]
417	  -- Apply `IsLocalization.lift_unique`: it suffices to show
418	  -- `g_f.hom ∘ algebraMap_{MvPoly→GmRing} = polyMap` on the MvPoly side.
419	  refine IsLocalization.lift_unique _ ?_
420	  change ∀ (x : MvPolynomial Unit kbar),
421	    (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop).hom.comp
422	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) x = (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _) x
423	  apply DFunLike.congr_fun
424	  -- Both sides are RingHoms `MvPoly Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. By `ringHom_ext`,
425	  -- it suffices to compare on `C r` (constants) and `X i` (variables).
426	  refine MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext (fun r => ?_) (fun i => ?_)
427	  · -- `C r`-case: by `Over.w f`, `g_f ∘ algebraMap_{kbar→GmRing} = structureRingMap`.
428	    have hwr := congrArg (fun (g : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ _) => g.hom r) hw
429	    simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom] at hwr
430	    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
431	    -- Now LHS = g_f.hom (algebraMap kbar GmRing r), RHS = structureRingMap.hom r
432	    exact hwr.symm
433	  · -- `X i`-case: `i : Unit`, so `i = ()`; then both sides reduce to `(toFun f).val`.
434	    obtain ⟨⟩ := i
435	    simp [gmHomEquiv_toFun]
436	
437	/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
438	`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
439	plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
440	private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
442	    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
443	  intro u
444	  apply Units.ext
445	  -- Stage the proof at the CommRingCat morphism level.
446	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
447	        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
448	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val
449	  -- Unfold `invFun` to expose `T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ofHom liftedMap)` as the
450	  -- underlying scheme morphism.
451	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
452	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
453	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
454	        (IsLocalization.Away.lift
455	          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop).hom
456	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X ())) = u.val
457	  -- Step (a): morphism equality `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ invFun(u).left.appTop = ofHom liftedMap`
458	  -- via `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`, `← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc`,
459	  -- `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop`, `Iso.inv_hom_id`, `Category.comp_id`.
460	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
461	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
462	  -- Step (b)+(c): conclude via `IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`.
463	  simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
464	
465	/-- Naturality of `homEquiv`: pre-composition by `φ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over (Spec k̄)` corresponds
466	to `Units.map` of `φ.left.appTop` on the units side. Reduces to `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`
467	and the unit-element transfer through `IsLocalization.Away.lift` and the functor's `map` action. -/
468	private lemma gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
469	    {T T' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} (f : T ⟶ T') (g : T' ⟶ Gm kbar) :
470	    gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T (f ≫ g) =
471	      (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).map f.op (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T' g) := by
472	  apply Units.ext
473	  simp only [gmHomEquiv_toFun, gmHomFunctor, Functor.comp_map, Units.coe_map,
474	    IsUnit.unit_spec, GrpCat.hom_ofHom, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
475	  rfl
476	
477	/-- The `RepresentableBy` witness exhibiting `Gm kbar` as a representing object for the
478	units-of-global-sections functor. Per-`T` bijection is `gmHomEquiv_toFun` / `gmHomEquiv_invFun`;
479	round-trip identities split into `gmHomEquiv_left_inv` / `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` (named
480	substantive sorries) and naturality `homEquiv_comp` (named substantive sorry). The deep
481	Mathlib-gap content per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy — no
482	`Spec(Localization.Away)` analogue of `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` in Mathlib. -/
483	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor_representableBy (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
484	    (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar) where
485	  homEquiv {T} :=
486	    { toFun := gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T
487	      invFun := gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T
488	      left_inv := gmHomEquiv_left_inv kbar T
489	      right_inv := gmHomEquiv_right_inv kbar T }
490	  homEquiv_comp := gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp kbar
491	
492	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
493	
494	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
495	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
496	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
497	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection).
498	
499	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
500	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with

502	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) :=
503	  GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)
504	
505	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
506	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
507	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
508	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
509	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
510	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
511	
512	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
513	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
514	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
515	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
516	  η[Gm kbar]

8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
13	
14	* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	set_option linter.style.setOption false
28	
29	universe u w
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
32	
33	noncomputable section
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-! ### Project-local Mathlib supplement — MvPolynomial as standard smooth algebra
152	
153	This subsection ships an axiom-clean construction of the canonical submersive
154	presentation of `MvPolynomial ι R` as an `R`-algebra (no relations, generators
155	indexed by `ι`). Mathlib does not ship a direct instance
156	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)`
157	(per iter-182 prover scout); we build it here from the underlying
158	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` machinery. This is a pure
159	`Mathlib`-supplement: it depends only on `Mathlib`, and is independent
160	of the rest of the project.
161	-/
162	
163	/-- The canonical `Generators` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` indexed by `α`,
164	with `val := MvPolynomial.X` and section `σ' := id`. -/
165	noncomputable def mvPolyGenerators (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
166	    Algebra.Generators R (MvPolynomial α R) α :=
167	  Algebra.Generators.ofSurjective MvPolynomial.X <|
168	    fun s => ⟨s, MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left_apply s⟩
169	
170	/-- The canonical `Presentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` with no relations
171	(`σ = PEmpty`) and generators indexed by `α`. The kernel of the natural
172	`aeval X` map is `⊥` since `aeval X` is the identity. -/
173	noncomputable def mvPolyPresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
174	    Algebra.Presentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
175	  __ := mvPolyGenerators R α
176	  relation := PEmpty.elim
177	  span_range_relation_eq_ker := by
178	    simp only [Set.range_eq_empty, Ideal.span_empty]
179	    rw [Algebra.Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val]
180	    change ⊥ = RingHom.ker (MvPolynomial.aeval (R := R) (MvPolynomial.X : α → MvPolynomial α R))
181	    rw [MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]
182	    ext x
183	    simp [RingHom.mem_ker]
184	
185	/-- The canonical `PreSubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
186	The differential is on the zero module `PEmpty → P.Ring`. -/
187	noncomputable def mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
188	    (α : Type w) :
189	    Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
190	  __ := mvPolyPresentation R α
191	  map := PEmpty.elim
192	  map_inj := fun a _ _ => PEmpty.elim a
193	
194	/-- The canonical `SubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
195	The Jacobian is the determinant of the zero-sized matrix, which is `1` (a unit). -/
196	noncomputable def mvPolySubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
197	    (α : Type w) :
198	    Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
199	  __ := mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation R α
200	  jacobian_isUnit := by
201	    rw [Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation.jacobian_eq_jacobiMatrix_det]
202	    simp [Matrix.det_isEmpty]
203	
204	/-- **Mathlib supplement**: `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R` is `R`-standard smooth of
205	relative dimension `n`. Built from `mvPolySubmersivePresentation` with
206	dimension `Nat.card (Fin n) - Nat.card PEmpty = n`. -/
207	instance mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
208	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) :
209	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
210	  (mvPolySubmersivePresentation R (Fin n)).isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension <| by
211	    simp [Algebra.Presentation.dimension]
212	
213	/-! ### `GeometricallyIrreducible` instance -/
214	
215	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
216	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
217	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
218	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
219	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
220	  sorry
221	
222	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
223	
224	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
225	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
226	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
227	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
228	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
229	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
230	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
231	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
232	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
233	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
234	  fun i => by
235	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
236	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
237	
238	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
239	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
240	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
241	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
242	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
243	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
244	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
245	
246	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
247	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
248	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
249	
250	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
251	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
252	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
253	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
254	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
255	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
256	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
257	    (m := ![1, 1])
258	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
259	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
260	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
261	    (by
262	      classical
263	      intro p hp
264	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
265	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
266	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
267	        have := hp
268	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
269	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
270	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
271	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
272	        exact this
273	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
274	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
275	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
276	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
277	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
278	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
279	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
280	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
281	        by_contra h
282	        push Not at h
283	        apply hd_ne
284	        ext k
285	        fin_cases k
286	        · simpa using h.1
287	        · simpa using h.2
288	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
289	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
290	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
291	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
292	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
293	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
294	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
295	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
296	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
297	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
298	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
299	
300	-- NOTE iter-197: relocated to AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean per BareScheme smoothness-relocation refactor.
301	
302	end AlgebraicGeometry
303

7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 2): the chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 2** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships the
13	load-bearing chart-ring iso
14	
15	```
16	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i)
17	  ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar
18	```
19	
20	together with the `kbar`-algebra preservation lemma. The forward direction is via
21	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`; the inverse via
22	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i = isLocalizationElem`.
23	
24	Upstream stratum: `BareScheme`. Downstream strata: `Points`, `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
39	
40	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
41	def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
42	  | 0 => 1
43	  | 1 => 0
44	
45	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
46	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
47	
48	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
49	  fin_cases i <;> decide
50	
51	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
52	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
53	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
54	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
55	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
56	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
57	
58	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
59	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
60	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
61

63	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by

144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
